title: Public holidays | Employment New Zealand
content:
Employees have minimum rights that apply to public holidays.
- Public holidays and anniversary dates Employees have minimum rights that apply to public holidays.

## Public holidays and anniversary dates
Employees have minimum rights that apply to public holidays.
- Previous years: Public holidays and anniversary dates New Zealand public holidays and anniversary dates for 2010 to 2025.

## Previous years: Public holidays and anniversary dates
New Zealand public holidays and anniversary dates for 2010 to 2025.
- Managing public holidays as an employer Your employees are entitled to a paid day off if a public holiday falls on a normal working day for them.

## Managing public holidays as an employer
Your employees are entitled to a paid day off if a public holiday falls on a normal working day for them.
- Public holidays rights for employees An employee is entitled to a paid day off if a public holiday falls on a day they would otherwise be working.

## Public holidays rights for employees
An employee is entitled to a paid day off if a public holiday falls on a day they would otherwise be working.
- Transferring a public holiday An employer and employee can make an agreement to transfer an entire public holiday so that it is observed on another day.

## Transferring a public holiday
An employer and employee can make an agreement to transfer an entire public holiday so that it is observed on another day.
- Alternative holidays An alternative holiday (sometimes called a ‘day in lieu’) is a day off to take at another time. Employees get an alternative holiday when they work on a public holiday that falls on a normal working day for them.

## Alternative holidays
An alternative holiday (sometimes called a ‘day in lieu’) is a day off to take at another time. Employees get an alternative holiday when they work on a public holiday that falls on a normal working day for them.
- When a public holiday falls on a weekend When a public holiday falls on a Saturday or Sunday, an employee’s public holiday may be moved to the following Monday – or in some cases Tuesday.

## When a public holiday falls on a weekend
When a public holiday falls on a Saturday or Sunday, an employee’s public holiday may be moved to the following Monday – or in some cases Tuesday.
- Restricted trading days for shops On 3 and a half days each year, almost all shops must close. But some shops can open with conditions, or they have an area exemption or Easter Sunday local policy.

## Restricted trading days for shops
On 3 and a half days each year, almost all shops must close. But some shops can open with conditions, or they have an area exemption or Easter Sunday local policy.
- Working and trading on Easter Sunday In addition to the restricted trading rules, local council policies may also apply to shops wishing to trade on Easter Sunday.

## Working and trading on Easter Sunday
In addition to the restricted trading rules, local council policies may also apply to shops wishing to trade on Easter Sunday.
